question: [File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.use(compression())] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BREACH attack against the compression middleware enabled on line 78, by making repeated requests with varying query parameters to extract secrets from compressed API responses via side-channel analysis of response sizes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: metricsHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user access the Prometheus metrics endpoint at {uriPath}/metrics/ without credentials when config.metrics.config.authentication is false or not set, leaking internal performance data, route names, and request counts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: metricsHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a brute-force attack against the metrics endpoint Basic Auth in authenticate() since there is no rate limiting or lockout mechanism protecting the metrics credentials?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: metricsHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted content-length header to inflate requestSizeHistogram metrics, causing misleading monitoring data that masks real attack traffic patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: metricsHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the server with requests to inflate api_all_request_in_processing_total (inFlightCounter) metrics, causing false alerting or masking legitimate traffic anomalies in the monitoring system?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: metricsHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user access the metrics endpoint to enumerate all registered API route paths via the api_request counter labels, revealing the complete internal routing structure including undocumented endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: createTerminus()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly poll /health/readiness to trigger the readinessCheck database query (select true from address_book limit 1) and exhaust the database connection pool, causing a denial-of-service for legitimate API requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: createTerminus()] Can an unprivileged external user access /health/liveness without any authentication to confirm the server is alive and gather timing information useful for targeted denial-of-service attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: createTerminus()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests to /health/readiness to cause the terminus health check to fail, triggering an unintended graceful shutdown via the onShutdown handler that closes the database pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: initializePool()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the PostgreSQL connection pool by sending many concurrent slow requests, since the pool max connections is bounded by config.db.pool.maxConnections and there is no per-user connection limit enforced at the server level?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: initializePool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rejectUnauthorized: false TLS option in dbpool.js to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the database connection if the attacker is on the same network segment, intercepting query results?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress`)] Can an unprivileged external user send a specially crafted EVM address or alias in the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter to trigger unexpected behavior in account lookup, such as returning data for a different account due to alias resolution ambiguity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/accounts`)] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all accounts by repeatedly calling getAccounts with different pagination cursors, extracting the full account list from the mirror node without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/balances`)] Can an unprivileged external user query getBalances with timestamp parameters to reconstruct historical balance snapshots for any account, enabling financial surveillance without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber/transactions`)] Can an unprivileged external user send an extremely large serialNumber value in the path parameter to getNftTransferHistoryRequest to trigger an integer overflow or unexpected database query behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/transactions/:transactionIdOrHash`)] Can an unprivileged external user send a very long or specially crafted transactionIdOrHash path parameter to getTransactionsByIdOrHash to trigger a ReDoS vulnerability in the transaction ID parsing regex?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/topics/:topicId/messages/:sequenceNumber`)] Can an unprivileged external user send a negative or zero sequenceNumber to getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest to trigger unexpected database query behavior or return unintended records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/topics/messages/:consensusTimestamp`)] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed consensusTimestamp (e.g., with special characters or extreme values) to getMessageByConsensusTimestamp to trigger a SQL injection or type coercion error that leaks database internals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: app.getExt(`${apiPrefix}/schedules/:scheduleId`)] Can an unprivileged external user send a non-numeric or oversized scheduleId to getScheduleById to trigger an unhandled error that leaks internal error messages via handleError's shouldReturnMessage logic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an unregistered route to trigger the NotFoundError thrown in responseHandler when responseData is undefined, and observe whether the error message leaks internal routing information?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the Link header value by crafting a request that results in a linksNext value containing a malicious URL, since linkNextHeaderValue directly interpolates res.locals.responseData.links.next without sanitization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the path-based header merging in responseHandler (headers.path[path]) to receive unexpected response headers if the path matching logic can be manipulated via URL encoding or normalization tricks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: requestLogger()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that requestLogger logs req.ip (which is derived from X-Forwarded-For due to trust proxy: true) to inject fake IP addresses into server logs, enabling log poisoning or log injection attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: requestLogger()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted originalUrl containing newline characters to inject fake log entries via the logger.info call in responseHandler that logs req.originalUrl?

[File: 'File Name: rest/server.js -> Scope: '] [Function: httpContext.middleware] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a context isolation failure in express-http-context to read or overwrite the requestId or userLimitLabel of a concurrent request, bypassing per-user rate limits set by authHandler?
